name: Royal Kitchen
tagline: Chinatown's legendary baked manapua — the char siu pork buns have been a local staple since 1975.
mustOrder: baked char siu manapua — the signature, golden and sweet
pepeiao — a local-style sweet bread ear pastry
parking: Chinatown Cultural Plaza parking; metered street parking
talkStory: Inside Chinatown Cultural Plaza since 1975, Royal Kitchen created what many consider Hawaii's definitive baked manapua. Unlike the steamed Chinese original, Royal Kitchen's char siu bao has a sweet, golden baked crust -- a distinctly local adaptation that became its own tradition. Founder Henry Chang's recipe hasn't changed in nearly 50 years. The pepeiao (ear-shaped pastry) is an old-school Honolulu treat that's almost impossible to find elsewhere. Cash only, and regulars buy by the dozen.
